message:
ci(bench): cross-platform ORT baseline + summaries explain the numbers

The CI bench summary printed raw textsift latency numbers with no
context — readers couldn't tell if "11.6 ms" was fast or slow. Two
fixes:

1) ORT baseline now runs on every OS family (Linux/Mac/Windows),
   not just Linux. Uses a new tests/native/bench-onnx/ort-baseline.js
   that finds the model in textsift's own on-disk cache (the e2e
   integration test populates it earlier in the same job — no
   manual staging needed). Adds onnxruntime-node to root devDeps.

2) Per-OS step summary and the cross-OS aggregate both now lead
   with "textsift vs ORT Node CPU at T=32, with explicit speedup".
   Includes a "how to read these numbers" preamble (16ms feels
   instant; 50ms interactive; 100ms threshold; 250ms+ user-visible)
   and a "feel" column on the e2e table so readers map raw ms to
   user perception.
   The aggregate also explicitly names what the comparison does NOT
   cover — PyTorch CUDA on datacenter GPUs, PyTorch CPU/MPS in the
   Python ecosystem — so we don't oversell the lane textsift is in.

Local smoke (Mac, M2 Pro): textsift Metal-direct 11.6 ms vs ORT
Node CPU 342.9 ms = 30× faster. That's the kind of headline a
summary should lead with, not a context-free "11.6 ms".
